title: 9 Herbal Tinctures to Fight Periodontal Disease Symptoms

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for periodontal disease because they have antimicrobial properties that help reduce bacteria in the mouth, preventing inflammation and infection of the gums.

Quercetin is good for periodontal disease because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off bacteria in the gums, promoting healthy gum tissue and preventing further damage.

Tannins are good for periodontal disease because they have antimicrobial properties that help kill the bacteria that cause inflammation and infection in the gums, reducing the risk of gum disease and promoting healthy gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has been used for a long time to help with periodontal disease. This is because it has strong antibacterial properties that can kill the bacteria that cause gum disease. These bacteria can cause inflammation and infection in the gums, which can lead to pain, bleeding, and even tooth loss. The antibacterial properties in tea tree oil can help to reduce the amount of bacteria in the mouth, which can help to reduce the symptoms of periodontal disease. Additionally, tea tree oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the swelling and redness in the gums. This can make it easier to manage the symptoms of periodontal disease. Tea tree oil can be used as a tincture, which is a liquid extract of the oil, and applied directly to the gums with a cotton swab. It can also be added to mouthwash or toothpaste to help kill bacteria and reduce inflammation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures that help with periodontal disease, start by harvesting fresh or drying Calendula flowers. If using fresh flowers, gently wash them with clean water to remove dirt. Next, combine 1 part of Calendula flowers with 5 parts of high-proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. For example, if using a 1-liter jar, add 200 grams of Calendula flowers and 1 liter of vodka. Close the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily. This process allows the Calendula's active compounds to infuse into the solvent. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. This Calendula tincture can be used topically to help reduce inflammation and promote healing in gums affected by periodontal disease.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal tinctures for periodontal disease

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for periodontal disease because it has powerful antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that cause gum infections and damage to the teeth and gums.

Cichoric acid is good for periodontal disease because it has antibacterial properties that can help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, thereby preventing the infection and inflammation associated with gum disease.

Tannins are good for periodontal disease because they have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce plaque formation and inflammation in the gums, thereby preventing the progression of periodontitis.

Flavonoids are good for periodontal disease because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and kill bacteria in the gums, promoting healthy gum tissue and preventing further damage.

Polyphenols are good for periodontal disease because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight bacteria in the gums, promoting healthy healing and preventing further damage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is a liquid extract made from the roots of the Echinacea plant. It's been used for centuries to help boost the immune system and fight off infections. When it comes to periodontal disease, Echinacea purpurea tincture may be beneficial because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, which are common symptoms of periodontal disease. Echinacea purpurea tincture also has antimicrobial properties, which can help kill bacteria that cause periodontal disease. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Echinacea purpurea tincture may help prevent the progression of periodontal disease and promote healing of the gums. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tincture may help increase the production of white blood cells, which are an important part of the immune system and can help fight off infections that can lead to periodontal disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures that help with periodontal disease, start by gathering clean, dry Calendula flowers. Use a sharp knife to chop the flowers into small pieces, then place them in a clean glass jar. Next, pour a neutral-tasting liquid, such as vodka or glycerin, over the flowers, making sure they are completely covered. The general ratio is one part herbs to three parts liquid, but adjust the amount to your preference. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process allows the herbs to infuse their properties into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken internally in small amounts, usually a few drops diluted in water, or applied topically to affected areas of the mouth and gums, to help reduce inflammation and promote healing. However, please note that this tincture is not a replacement for medical treatment and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for periodontal disease

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid helps prevent periodontal disease because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and fight off harmful bacteria in the gums.

Quercetin is good for periodontal disease because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off bacteria in the gums, preventing further damage and promoting healthy gum tissue.
Tannins are good for periodontal disease because they have antibacterial properties that can help kill the bacteria that cause inflammation and infection in the gums.

Polyphenols are good for periodontal disease because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bacteria growth and inflammation in the gums, promoting a healthier mouth and preventing further damage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, has been used for centuries to help with oral health issues. The tincture made from this herb is considered beneficial for periodontal disease because of its antibacterial properties. It contains compounds like carnosic acid and ursolic acid that can help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause infections in the gums. This can lead to a decrease in inflammation and swelling, making it easier to maintain healthy gums and teeth. Additionally, sage tincture has been shown to improve saliva production, which can help neutralize acids in the mouth and keep teeth clean. By reducing bacteria and improving saliva production, sage tincture may help prevent the progression of periodontal disease and promote a healthier mouth.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures that help with periodontal disease, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by gathering fresh or dried Calendula flowers, as they are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat periodontal disease. Next, combine one part of Calendula flowers with one part of a neutral-tasting carrier oil, such as grapeseed or sunflower oil, in a clean glass jar. Store the mixture in a cool, dark place and let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the oil extract the beneficial compounds from the flowers. After the steeping process, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to small glass dropper bottles and store them in the fridge to slow down the oxidation process. To use the tincture, mix a few drops with water and swish it around your mouth for 30 seconds before spitting it out, ideally after brushing your teeth and before meals to help prevent periodontal disease and promote oral health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tincture is good for periodontal disease because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The tincture has compounds like eucalyptol, which can fight bacteria that cause infections. When used as a mouthwash, it can help kill bacteria in the mouth and reduce plaque, a sticky film that forms on teeth. By reducing plaque and bacteria, it can help prevent the progression of periodontal disease. The tincture can also help to reduce gum inflammation and promote healing. This can help to prevent further damage to the gums and teeth, and may even help to reverse some of the damage that has already been done.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures that help with periodontal disease, you will need some dried Calendula flowers and a solvent like ethanol or glycerin. Start by measuring out a quantity of dried Calendula flowers - a general ratio is 1 part of flowers to 2-3 parts of solvent. Combine the flowers and solvent in a clean glass jar, making sure that the flowers are completely submerged in the liquid. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the solvent extract the active compounds from the flowers. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, squeezing the cheesecloth or filter to extract as much liquid as possible from the flowers. Discard the solids and transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Calendula tinctures have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria associated with periodontal disease. You can take the tincture as directed, usually by mixing a few drops in water or another liquid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is often used to help with periodontal disease. This is because the tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the gums. Ginkgo biloba also has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. These free radicals can lead to the breakdown of gum tissue and the loss of teeth. By reducing inflammation and protecting the gums, Ginkgo biloba tincture may help to prevent the progression of periodontal disease. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has been shown to improve blood flow to the gums, which can help to promote healing and prevent the formation of new plaque and tartar. This improved blood flow can also help to reduce the risk of tooth loss.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Calendula officinalis herbal tincture that may help with periodontal disease, you will need dried calendula flowers and a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin. Start by measuring out one part of dried calendula flowers and adding it to four parts of your chosen solvent in a clean glass jar. Stir well and seal the jar, then let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. This will allow the calendula to infuse into the solvent, creating a strong tincture. After the infusion time has passed, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. You can now use the calendula tincture as a mouthwash to help soothe and heal periodontal disease, by mixing a few drops with water and swishing it around your mouth before spitting it out. Some studies suggest that calendula's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties may help reduce plaque, prevent infection, and promote healing in the gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tincture, also known as aloe vera, has been used for many years to help with oral health issues. The tincture is made from the gel of the aloe plant, which contains many active ingredients that are good for the mouth. These ingredients, such as vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, help to soothe and calm the gums. When used as a mouthwash, aloe vera tincture can reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause periodontal disease. This helps to prevent the disease from getting worse and even promotes healing of damaged gums. The tincture also contains compounds that help to reduce plaque and prevent new bacteria from growing. Additionally, aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with periodontal disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures that help with periodontal disease, start by gathering dried Calendula flowers. Fill a clean glass jar about one-quarter full with the dried flowers. Next, pour high-proof vodka or ethanol over the flowers, making sure that the flowers are completely covered. The general ratio is one part flowers to two parts solvent. Secure the lid tightly and let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks. Shake the jar gently every day or two to help the solvent extract the beneficial properties from the flowers. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as directed. Calendula tincture has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe and protect gums, reducing the risk of periodontal disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, has been used for centuries to help with various health issues, including periodontal disease. The active compound in turmeric, called curcumin, has powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When used as a tincture, curcumin can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, which is a common symptom of periodontal disease. Curcumin also has antibacterial properties, which can help kill the bacteria that cause gum infections. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, turmeric tincture may help prevent the progression of periodontal disease and promote healthy gum tissue growth. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects on the body, which can help reduce the risk of other health problems, such as heart disease and diabetes, which are often linked to periodontal disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tincture that helps with periodontal disease, start by gathering dried Calendula flowers. You will need a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like olive or coconut oil, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Fill the jar with 1 part dried Calendula flowers to 2 parts carrier oil, making sure the flowers are completely covered. Close the lid and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. After this period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Next, add 1 part solvent to 2 parts plant mixture, and mix well. Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid in the fridge. To use, place a few drops directly on the affected gum tissue, 2-3 times a day. Calendula's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties can help reduce swelling, kill bacteria, and promote healing in periodontal disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sanguinaria canadensis tincture is a herbal remedy that may help with periodontal disease. The active compounds in Sanguinaria canadensis, such as benzylisoquinoline alkaloids, have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause gum disease and inflammation in the gums. Sanguinaria canadensis tincture may also help reduce bleeding and swelling in the gums, making it easier to maintain good oral hygiene. Additionally, the tincture may help reduce pain and discomfort associated with periodontal disease, making it a potential natural remedy for managing symptoms. The tincture may be used topically or taken internally to help address the underlying causes of periodontal disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tincture that helps with periodontal disease, start by gathering fresh Calendula flowers and drying them in a warm, dark place. Once dry, you can grind the flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, combine one part of the dried Calendula powder with one part of a high-proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar. Make sure the jar is filled to the top to minimize air exposure, which can cause spoilage. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the tincture extract the active properties from the Calendula. After the extraction period, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as directed by a healthcare professional or according to the label instructions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold, is a plant with anti-inflammatory properties that can help treat periodontal disease. The tincture made from this plant can be applied to the gums to reduce swelling and pain. The anti-inflammatory compounds in Calendula officinalis, such as flavonoids and triterpenoids, can help to reduce the redness and swelling of the gums, making it easier to clean and maintain good oral hygiene. Additionally, Calendula officinalis has antimicrobial properties that can help to kill bacteria that cause periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Calendula officinalis tincture can help to prevent the progression of periodontal disease and promote healthy gums.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Calendula officinalis herbal tincture that helps with periodontal disease, start by gathering dried Calendula flowers and a clean glass jar with a lid. Combine one part of dried Calendula flowers with one part of vodka in the jar, making sure the flowers are completely covered by the vodka. Close the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the vodka extract the beneficial properties from the flowers. After the extraction period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting liquid is the Calendula tincture, which can be used to help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections associated with periodontal disease. It is recommended to dilute the tincture with water before using it, typically with a ratio of 1-2 drops of the tincture per 8 ounces of water, and to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for periodontal disease.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a helpful addition to the treatment of periodontal disease, a condition that affects the gums and bone supporting the teeth. One key herb in addressing this issue is Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil. Its antimicrobial properties can help combat bacteria that contribute to the disease, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage.  Echinacea purpurea is another herb that can be beneficial in fighting periodontal disease. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it easier to maintain good oral hygiene.  Fixing periodontal disease is crucial because, left untreated, it can lead to tooth loss and other serious health issues. Periodontal disease has been linked to an increased risk of heart disease, diabetes, and even Alzheimer's disease. By addressing this condition, individuals can improve their oral health and overall well-being. They can also enjoy the benefits of a healthy smile, confident in their appearance, and better equipped to chew and digest food properly. This can lead to improved nutrition and energy levels, enabling individuals to live a more active and fulfilling life.
supplementary_best: For periodontal disease, the best combination of herbal tinctures is Melaleuca alternifolia (tea tree oil), Echinacea purpurea, and Curcuma longa. Melaleuca alternifolia has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat the infection and reduce swelling in the gums. Echinacea purpurea stimulates the immune system, helping the body fight off the disease and prevent it from spreading. Curcuma longa, or turmeric, contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. This combination of Melaleuca alternifolia and Curcuma longa provides a strong antimicrobial effect, while Echinacea purpurea helps the body's natural defenses. Together, they work to eliminate the infection, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in the gums.
supplementary_avoid: If you have periodontal disease, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures that can make bleeding or infection worse. One such herb is 'Comfrey' (Symphytum officinale). Comfrey contains compounds that can slow healing and cause more bleeding. Another herb to avoid is 'Dong Quai' (Angelica sinensis). Dong Quai can thin the blood and increase the risk of bleeding. 'Ginseng' (Panax ginseng) should also be avoided as it can increase blood pressure and make bleeding worse. 'Licorice root' (Glycyrrhiza glabra) can also be problematic because it can increase blood pressure and cause inflammation. 'Vitamin K' is often included in tinctures to help with bleeding, but taking too much can lead to blood clots and other complications. Finally, 'Garlic' (Allium sativum) can also be a problem as it can thin the blood and cause more bleeding. These herbs should be avoided or used with caution to prevent making periodontal disease worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel has antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to periodontal disease. Aloe vera gel can be applied directly to the gums with a cotton swab to soothe and calm the area. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain.  Diet is a crucial factor in maintaining healthy gums. Omega-3 fatty acids found in fish oil supplements can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to periodontal disease. Omega-3 fatty acids also have antibacterial properties that can help kill bacteria in the mouth.  Probiotics are beneficial bacteria that can help maintain a healthy balance of bacteria in the mouth. When this balance is disrupted, it can lead to periodontal disease. Probiotics can be found in foods like yogurt and kefir, or taken as a supplement.  Coconut oil has antimicrobial properties that can help kill bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to periodontal disease. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain. Coconut oil can be used as a mouthwash or applied directly to the gums with a cotton swab.  Dietary changes, such as increasing consumption of fruits and vegetables, can also help maintain healthy gums. Foods high in fiber, such as apples and carrots, can help clean the teeth and reduce inflammation in the gums. Foods high in antioxidants, such as berries and leafy greens, can help reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to periodontal disease.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with periodontal disease are more likely to experience other health problems due to the bacteria and inflammation in their mouths. These problems can be healed with medicinal herbs. For example, people with periodontal disease are more likely to have heart disease. The bacteria in their mouths can enter their bloodstream and cause inflammation in their hearts. Medicinal herbs like turmeric and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce this inflammation and heal the heart.  Another problem people with periodontal disease may experience is diabetes. The inflammation in their mouths can make it harder to control their blood sugar levels. Medicinal herbs like berberine and cinnamon have been shown to lower blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity, which can help manage diabetes.  People with periodontal disease may also be more likely to experience respiratory problems like chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). The bacteria in their mouths can be inhaled into their lungs and cause inflammation. Medicinal herbs like thyme and eucalyptus have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce this inflammation and heal the lungs.  In addition, people with periodontal disease may be more likely to experience digestive problems like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The inflammation in their mouths can cause inflammation in their digestive tracts, leading to symptoms like diarrhea and abdominal pain. Medicinal herbs like peppermint and chamomile have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce this inflammation and heal the digestive tract.
